mnemonics.title: Operator Precedence Order
mnemonics.content: PCKU: Parentheses > Kleene star > Concatenation > Union. Remember: 'Please (K)Check (C)Under' - like checking priority levels in operations

mnemonics.title: Pumping Lemma Steps
mnemonics.content: PLUC: 1) Pick string 2) Length exceeds pumping length 3) uvw decomposition 4) Contradiction when pumped. 'Pump Lemons Until Contradiction'

mnemonics.title: Closure Properties
mnemonics.content: RICK-M: Regular languages closed under Reverse, Intersection, Complement, Kleene star, Concatenation, Union, and Minus

mnemonics.title: Extended Operators
mnemonics.content: PQBDN: Plus (R+), Question (R?), Brackets ([abc]), Dot (.), Numbers ({n,m}). 'People Question Brackets Doing Numbers'

acronyms.usage: Operator precedence order in regex parsing
Core regex operators (Kleene star, ε, concatenation)

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a spinning asterisk (*) creating infinite loops around the previous character
Visualize train cars linked together - each regex element must follow in sequence
Picture a fork in the road - choose either path (a|b means path a or path b)
Think of a water pump - repeatedly 'pump' a section of string while maintaining pattern
Imagine a vending machine - [abc] lets you choose any one option (a, b, or c)

analogies.analogy: Like a pizza order: Specify exact ingredients (literals), optional toppings (R?), or 'any topping' (.)
Like a subway pass - allows unlimited rides (repetitions) of the same train (character)
A maze with rooms (states) and doors (transitions) - only certain paths lead to exit (acceptance)
Like a bicycle pump - if you can't inflate/repeat a section without breaking, it's not regular
